g.text: Paxos Safety: A New Proposer Must Adopt the Highest-Numbered Accepted Value
If any acceptor has accepted a value in an earlier round, the new
proposer is forced to propose that value — not its own. This rule
is what prevents two rounds from ever choosing different values.
Phase A — old proposer P1 runs n=5, A1 accepts "X" before A2/A3 vote, then P1 crashes
Phase B — new proposer P2 picks n=10 and WANTS to propose "Y"
Safety rule: among all values returned in Phase-1 promises, the new proposer adopts the one with the highest accepted-round n.
Without this rule, P1's partial accept of "X" + P2's accept of "Y" could let two rounds choose different values.
